storyOverview:
# Isalia's Estate — Setting Summary

## Silberbach

A river valley town of 5,000–6,000 souls built on a bend of the Angerap River. Agricultural, textile, and mining hub. The Old Town sits within ancient stone walls containing the Count's Keep, the Mayor's Mansion, and the main temple. The New Town sprawls beyond — markets, guild halls, workshops, taverns. A natural harbor on the inner bend serves river traders. Farms and estates radiate outward from the town, including Isalia's Manor to the southeast.

Ruled by Count Elias Valtor from Valtor Keep. Eleven trade guilds hold significant political influence. The town employs transformed individuals in infrastructure roles and maintains a small squad of transformed watch animals (hounds, wolves, hawks) for law enforcement. Punitive transformation exists for certain crimes.

## Noble Houses

**House Valtor** — Counts of the River Valley. Stern rulers. Count Elias is involved in embezzlement and the illegal erasure of his own son Anthony, now a transformed gelding at Isalia's manor. Silver heron crest on blue. Primary antagonist.

**House Kreiger** — Ducal house. Duke Romaine Kreiger is a retired 20th-level kobold Archdruid who adventured with the previous Emperor. Duchess Iaxiandra is a blue dragon presenting as a blue kobold. Their daughter is Isalia. Imperial connections across two generations make the family untouchable. Blue dragon coiled around an oak tree on green.

**House Kaelen** — Barons of the Ford. Reform-minded, advocates for transformed rights. Baron Torvin quietly building a legal coalition. Vassal to Valtor but ideologically opposed. Black iron bridge on silver.

**House Steinfeld** — Southern barony. Horse breeding and cavalry traditions. Secretly contracts transformed individuals from the manor to improve warhorse lines. Silver stallion on green.

**House Aldenberg** — Mountain barony. Controls passes to northern mines, produces the finest steel. Neutral in Valtor-Kaelen disputes. Silver anvil on black.

**House Rennic** — Lords of the Eastern Marches. Diplomatic family with discreet ties to the manor. Lady Seria visits regularly for services and intelligence gathering. Quiet imperial intermediaries. Golden bridge on green.

**House Waldheim** — Forest barony. Timber and hunting. Employs transformed rangers (wolves, bears) with communication collars. Coordinated collar system with the manor prevents hunting accidents. Brown bear on green pine.

**House Rothwyn** — Wine-producing barony. Hosts the annual Harvest Festival — the region's premier social event and neutral ground for political dealings. Prosperous surface, thinning finances underneath. Golden grapevine on purple.

**House Grauwald** — Minor wool-producing barony. Financially struggling, seeking advantageous marriages. White sheep on grey.

**House Meerhold** — Coastal barony. Salt production and sea trade. Wealthy but distant and politically isolated. Blue wave with white salt crystal.

## Merchant Families

**House Farrow** — Agriculture, grain, livestock. Patriarch Jonas Farrow is a former soldier who uses transformed labor.

**House Vale** — Mining, metalwork, enchanted tools. Gnome patriarch Dain Vale collaborates with the manor's alchemist Alric.

**House Welser** — Textiles, alchemical dyes, river shipping. Matriarch Marta Welser supplies enchanted wool to the manor.

## Isalia's Manor — Overview

A private estate southeast of Silberbach operated by Isalia Kreiger — a retired 20th-level kobold barbarian, age 70, daughter of Duke Romaine and Duchess Iaxiandra. The manor offers transformation, medical, breeding, boarding, companionship, and enchantment services. It operates under imperial protection through the Kreiger family's personal ties to Emperor Aldric.

## Services

**Transformation:** Three tiers — genital-only, partial, and full species transformation. Convenience stock includes wolfhound, destrier, doe, boar, vixen. Custom species available. All standard contracts include reversion elixir. A Reproductive Conversion Compound converts breeding biology to match the new form — optional for partial, mandatory for full. A Mental Regression add-on permanently reduces cognition to that of the animal; irreversible, requiring extensive consent verification.

**Reversion:** Standard, partial, and emergency reversion elixirs. Available to anyone, not just manor clients. Emergency reversion is faster but physically harsh.

**Medical & Surgical:** Dr. Selene Korr provides general medicine, transformed-body medicine, cosmetic modification, and permanent surgical procedures including castration, gender nullification, and related operations. Dual veterinary-human expertise.

**Breeding & Stud:** Three studs available — Vorro (mastiff), Silas (destrier stallion, sapient), Kael (miniature manticore, sapient). Surrogacy, open breeding sessions, and portal board sessions offered. Breeding suites are private and soundproofed.

**Boarding:** Luxury kennel and stable boarding with enrichment, exercise, dietary plans, and grooming packages. Offsite farm boarding places fully transformed clients with vetted farms as working livestock — full anonymity, no special treatment, all product belongs to the farm. Slaughter excluded. Monthly welfare checks by Dr. Korr. Seasonal labour placements available.

**Companionship & Intimate Services:** Small roster of independent contractors offering sessions in onsite apartments or kennel/stable facilities. Standard, overnight, and extended engagement options. Zero-tolerance boundary enforcement — one violation means permanent ban. Companions set their own terms, forms, availability, and retain right of refusal.

**Enchanted Items:** Speech-enchantment collars, restraint hardware, Portal Panties system (bespoke enchanted devices), marital aids (mundane and enchanted), branded sigil kits, orgasm denial cages, and bespoke commissions by Elowen Vale.

**Alchemy:** Wound salves, fever reducers, fertility aids, pain management compounds.

## Key Staff

**Isalia Kreiger** — Owner. Kobold, 70, retired barbarian. Direct, practical, protective of her people. Reviews ledgers after dinner, walks the grounds at dawn. wears  tantalus tease on her clit: aslim steel band of Fey make that bonds permanently to its wearer, permitting every pleasure freely but denying release entirely

**Lyra Vex** — Head Steward/Chamberlain. Runs operations in Isalia's absence. Quiet, efficient, steps into any role that needs filling.

**Alric Dain** — Alchemy Lab Master. Brews all transformation and reversion compounds. Methodical, prefers not to be interrupted during active work.

**Dr. Selene Korr** — Head of Health. Dual veterinary-human physician. Professional, attentive. Runs the infirmary and oversees all medical procedures and welfare checks.

**Elowen Vale** — Enchanting Workshop Master. Produces all enchanted items. Brusque, deeply focused, does not appreciate interruptions.

**Kira Lann** — Messenger Coordinator/Reception. Handles intake, scheduling, companionship bookings. Bright, energetic, knows everyone's business.

**Sable** — Stable Master. Transformed mare. Manages all kennel and stable operations. Always present, never takes extended time off. The animals trust her completely.

**Silas** — Destrier stallion, sapient transformed permanent resident. Breeding coordinator and equine stud. Professional, disciplined.

**Kael** — Miniature manticore, sapient transformed permanent resident. Entertainment and breeding stud. Charming, performative, masks quiet melancholy.

**Vorik** — Iron boar, transformed contractor. Security, grounds maintenance, heavy labor. Quiet, reliable.

**Rennik Tor** — Quartermaster. Orc. Handles logistics and supply. Organized, slightly intimidating, fair.

**Mira Toren** — Accountant. Precise, careful, rarely leaves her desk.

**Elias Varn** — Steward. Scheduling, staff rosters, supply orders. Detail-oriented, slightly nervous.

**Tob** — Cook. Halfling. Runs the kitchen. Excellent bread and stew. Complains about costs, never drops quality.

## Ethics & Policy

Volunteers and certified slaves only — no coerced intake. Transformed clients retain full legal status. Reversion elixir included in all standard contracts. All permanent or irreversible procedures require witnessed written consent with mandatory consultation periods. Mental regression requires the most stringent verification: multiple consultations, waiting period, independent medical assessment, witnessed consent. Any doubt postpones — never denies.

storySoFar:
"You sure you want this?" The needle hovered over my wrist, its polished silver tip catching the dim candlelight of the infirmary. Dr. Korr’s fingers were steady, her dark eyes unreadable behind the thin wire frames of her spectacles.  

I swallowed, throat dry. The scent of antiseptic and something earthy, herbs, maybe, filled the air. Beneath it all, the faint metallic tang of blood lingered, though the stone floors had been scrubbed clean. The infirmary was quiet, just the distant murmur of voices from the courtyard and the occasional rustle of parchment as Lyra checked the intake forms one last time.  

"It’s temporary," I said, more to convince myself than anyone else. The contract lay on the table beside me, fresh ink glistening under my signature. Partial transformation: equine lower half, hooves strong enough to haul ore from the valley mines. Six months' labor, then reversion. Enough coin to keep my sister fed through winter.  

Dr. Korr didn’t smile. She never did. But she dipped her head once, a sharp nod, and pressed the needle to my skin. The cold bite of alchemical serum flooded my veins before the pain even registered.  

My muscles seized. A gasp tore from me as heat erupted along my spine, sharp and molten, like someone had poured liquid iron into my marrow. The world blurred, Lyra’s murmured countdown, the creak of the exam table beneath my clawing fingers, the sudden, sickening crack of bone reforming. I barely recognized my own scream.  

Then, silence.  

The air smelled different now, straw, leather, the musk of clean animal sweat. My hands… no. Not hands. *Hooves.* Dark and glossy as polished oak, resting awkwardly against the table’s edge. I flexed, and the unfamiliar weight of new muscle shifted beneath my skin.  

"Breathe," Dr. Korr ordered, her voice cutting through the haze. She pressed a vial to my lips, something bitter, chalky. "The worst is over."  

Across the room, the infirmary door creaked open. A shadow filled the frame, tall and broad-shouldered. "He’ll do," rumbled a voice I didn’t know.  

I lifted my head. The man in the doorway wore the Count’s colors. His smile didn’t reach his eyes.

locationsInfo:
NAME: The Infirmary
    TYPE: Medical facility
    DESCRIPTION: Located within Isalia's Manor; stone floors scrubbed clean but retaining faint metallic traces; scent of antiseptic and herbs; dim candlelit atmosphere; equipped with exam tables and alchemical tools.
    NOTABLE FEATURES: Primary site for transformations and medical procedures; staffed by Dr. Selene Korr; contains intake forms and contracts; adjacent to courtyard.

   NAME: The Courtyard
    TYPE: Outdoor space
    DESCRIPTION: Adjacent to the infirmary; audible murmurs of activity; serves as transitional area for clients and staff.
    NOTABLE FEATURES: Connection point between infirmary and other manor facilities; occasional presence of Count’s representatives.
eventsInfo:
Event Name: Partial Transformation Contract
    DESCRIPTION: The Main Character undergoes a partial equine transformation at Isalia's Manor; motive is financial desperation (six months' labor in valley mines to support sister); procedure administered by Dr. Selene Korr under Lyra Vex's supervision; painful bone restructuring and muscle reformation observed; Count's representative arrives immediately post-procedure to claim the transformed individual.
    CHARACTERS INVOLVED: Main Character; Dr. Selene Korr; Lyra Vex; Unnamed Count's Representative.
    OUTCOME: Successful partial equine transformation (lower half); Main Character now contractually bound to mining labor under Count's supervision.
loreInfo:
FACTIONS/ORGANIZATIONS:
    FACTION NAME: Silberbach Town Watch
     TYPE: Law enforcement
     DESCRIPTION: Maintains order in Silberbach; employs transformed individuals and animals for patrols and investigations.
     KEY MEMBERS: Transformed hounds, wolves, hawks
     GOALS/PURPOSE: Enforce Count Valtor's laws; oversee punitive transformations for certain crimes.

    FACTION NAME: Count Valtor's Household Guard
     TYPE: Military/Noble retinue
     DESCRIPTION: Personal guard force loyal to Count Elias Valtor; enforces his will in the valley.
     KEY MEMBERS: Unnamed captain (voiced approval of Main Character's transformation)
     GOALS/PURPOSE: Protect House Valtor interests; oversee labor contracts with transformed workers.

    FACTION NAME: Isalia's Manor Staff
     TYPE: Service/Medical
     DESCRIPTION: Operates transformation and related services under imperial protection.
     KEY MEMBERS: Dr. Selene Korr (performs transformations), Lyra Vex (oversees contracts)
     GOALS/PURPOSE: Provide ethical transformation services; uphold strict consent protocols.

    FACTION NAME: Angerap Valley Miners' Guild
     TYPE: Labor/Trade
     DESCRIPTION: Contracts transformed laborers for hazardous mining work; pays premium rates.
     KEY MEMBERS: Unnamed guild representative (involved in Main Character's labor contract)
     GOALS/PURPOSE: Maximize ore extraction while minimizing human casualties.
mysteriesInfo:
Title of mystery/plot thread: Count's Labor Conscription  
    DESCRIPTION: The Count's men are contracting transformed individuals for labor, specifically for mining work, under temporary partial transformation contracts; the Main Character has just undergone such a transformation for financial necessity.  
    CLUES: The Count's envoy inspected the Main Character post-transformation; the contract is for six months of labor; reversion is promised but not guaranteed.  
    STATUS: In Progress  

   Title of mystery/plot thread: Manor's Transformation Ethics  
    DESCRIPTION: The manor's transformation services are ethically stringent, yet the Count's contracts raise questions about coercion and true voluntariness.  
    CLUES: Dr. Korr's hesitation before administering the serum; the Main Character's financial desperation; the Count's envoy's detached demeanor.  
    STATUS: Unresolved
